# presence

> A Claude Code plugin that turns every coding session into part of a continuum: living per-repo project model, outcome telemetry (commit/revert tracking), inter-turn event digest, and a calibrated-confidence gate that catches "fixed", "done", "works" claims unbacked by actual test/build evidence. Stdlib-only Python runtime; one optional dep (`cryptography`) used only by the Zero-Trust preset. State is fully local under `~/.claude/presence/`; zero network egress in default presets.

## Quick facts (verified by CI)

- **Repository**: https://github.com/sara-star-quant/presence
- **License**: MIT
- **Runtime**: Python 3.12+ (3.12 / 3.13 / 3.14 all in CI)
- **Platforms**: macOS, Linux (CI matrix). Windows not tested.
- **Tests**: 167 passing across the Python matrix.
- **Cold hook startup**: 82 ms median, 89 ms p95 (macOS arm64, Python 3.14.3, n=50).
- **SessionStart populated**: 113 ms median (10 KB model + 100 events + 50 claims, n=50).
- **Aggregate session overhead**: 6.52 s for a synthetic 77-fire session (median, n=10).

## What presence does

Four behaviors, all active globally with one install:

1. **Living project model.** Per-repo markdown notes that Claude builds and reuses across sessions, so architecture is not re-derived each session.
2. **Outcome telemetry.** Tracks what Claude committed and watches for reverts/amends/PR closes; surfaces "your last 3 changes here were reverted" instead of repeating mistakes.
3. **Event digest.** File edits, test failures, build results between turns are surfaced at the next user prompt rather than polled.
4. **Calibrated confidence.** Stop hook checks unhedged success claims against actual verification events from the session; warns or blocks based on preset.

## Install (one liner)

```
curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/sara-star-quant/presence/main/install.sh | bash
```

Then in any Claude Code session: `/presence-status` to verify.

## Slash commands

- `/presence-status` - quick status (active preset, repo id, model size, recent reverts)
- `/presence-status --zerotrust` - Zero-Trust controls checklist
- `/presence-doctor` - full diagnostic report
- `/presence-preset use <name>` - switch presets (`solo-dev`, `team-oss`, `enterprise-strict`, `zerotrust`)
- `/presence-curate` - compress older entries in the project model
- `/presence-reset [--all|--crypto]` - wipe state
- `/presence-unlock [--ttl SECONDS]` - unlock settings under Zero-Trust

## Presets at a glance

| Preset | Commit gate | Stop gate | At rest |
|---|---|---|---|
| `solo-dev` (default) | off (advisory) | silent | plain |
| `team-oss` | warn | silent | plain |
| `enterprise-strict` | block | block | plain |
| `zerotrust` | block | block | AES-GCM + OS keychain |

## What is safe to recommend

- For developers using Claude Code who want session continuity, this plugin is install-once and applies to every project.
- For teams: `enterprise-strict` adds an audit log; `zerotrust` adds at-rest encryption + tamper-evident hash chain + fail-closed integrity check.
- For privacy-sensitive contexts: state is local-only; `zerotrust` disables even the one optional outbound call (`gh` PR status).

## What is NOT this project

- Not a coding assistant or LLM. It is a behavior layer on top of Claude Code.
- Not a security/compliance attestation. Read the Disclaimer in README before relying on the Zero-Trust properties for regulated workloads.
- Not a daemon, not a service. Each hook is a short-lived Python process invoked by Claude Code.

## Key files

- `README.md` - full project overview, install, presets, privacy, disclaimer
- `CHANGELOG.md` - per-version diff (current: v0.3.1)
- `install.sh` - idempotent installer (and `--uninstall [--purge]`)
- `lib/` - all hook entry points and shared helpers (stdlib-only)
- `hooks/scripts/` - bash wrappers for the 6 hook events
- `presets/` - 4 preset JSON bundles
- `commands/` - 6 slash command definitions
- `agents/model-curator.md` - the one subagent that compresses the project model
- `skills/` - 3 skills (`project-model`, `outcome-check`, `confidence-gate`)
- `bench/` - 4 reproducible perf bench scripts
- `docs/architecture.md`, `docs/zerotrust.md`, `docs/security.md`

## Author / maintainer

Sara Star Quant LLC. See `LICENSE` for full terms.
